title: PERF review: boxing, SOCKS5, alloc — no findings
description:
Performance review of core/src/transport.rs SOCKS5 proxy implementation. Areas checked:

1. WsSink/WsStream boxing: Box allocated once per connection in ws_upgrade_and_auth; vtable dispatch per message is immaterial at human chat speed. No action needed.

2. SOCKS5 handshake on reconnect: connection_manager correctly re-runs full SOCKS5 + WS auth on every reconnect iteration. This is correct behavior, not a bug.

3. String allocations in parse_proxy_addr / parse_relay_addr: .to_owned() called once per connection attempt. Unavoidable; strings needed as owned values for Socks5Stream::connect. No unnecessary clones.

4. Async task structure: All I/O (Socks5Stream::connect, TcpStream::connect) is async. build_connector() is synchronous but does zero I/O. No blocking operations in the async path.

5. TLS connector construction: build_connector() called once per connection attempt. In the common path (accept_invalid_certs=false) it returns Ok(None) with zero allocation. In the dev-only insecure path it builds a TlsConnector once per reconnect — acceptable.

Only real finding filed separately as nie-gcwe.4 (double-parse in parse_incoming).
close_reason: Fixed by IPv6 bracket handling implementation
